de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.object_model.impl

Class SymbolicValue

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.object_model.impl.AbstractElement
      • de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.object_model.impl.SymbolicValue
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ISymbolicElement, ISymbolicValue

    public class SymbolicValue
extends AbstractElement
implements ISymbolicValue
    Default implementation of ISymbolicValue.
    Author:
    Martin Hentschel

      Returns the human readable array index or null if a program variable is represented..

      Returns the array start index.

      Returns the optional condition under which this value is valid.
      String getConditionString()
      Returns the optional condition under which this value is valid as human readable String.
      String getName()
      Returns a human readable name.

      Returns the represented IProgramVariable or null if an array index is represented.
      String getProgramVariableString()
      Returns the represented IProgramVariable as human readable String or null if an array index is represented.

      Returns the type of the value.
      String getTypeString()
      Returns the type of the value as human readable string.

      Returns the value of the represented variable.

      Returns the value of the represented variable as human readable String.

      Checks if an array index is represented.

      Checks if an array range is represented.

      • toString

        public String toString()
        Overrides:
        toString in class Object

      • getCondition

        public Term getCondition()

        Returns the optional condition under which this value is valid.

        The condition should be null by default. Only in rare cases, e.g. path condition is not strong enough to describe the path completely, is a condition is provided.

        Specified by:
        getCondition in interface ISymbolicValue
        Returns:
        The optional condition under which this value is valid.

      • getConditionString

        public String getConditionString()

        Returns the optional condition under which this value is valid as human readable String.

        The condition should be null by default. Only in rare cases, e.g. path condition is not strong enough to describe the path completely, is a condition is provided.

        Specified by:
        getConditionString in interface ISymbolicValue
        Returns:
        The optional condition under which this value is valid as human readable String.

        Returns the human readable array index or null if a program variable is represented..

        Returns:
        The human readable array index or null if a program variable is represented..
